data.content:
### Enable Corepack and Install pnpm Dependencies

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/ecommerce/bigcommerce

This command enables Corepack, a tool to manage Node.js package manager versions, then activates pnpm and uses it to install all project dependencies. It ensures the correct package manager is used for the Catalyst project setup.

```bash
corepack enable pnpm && pnpm install
```

--------------------------------

### Pin Package Manager Version with Corepack in package.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/builds/configure-a-build

This configuration snippet for `package.json` demonstrates how to use Corepack to pin a specific version of a package manager, such as pnpm. This experimental Node.js feature allows projects to ensure consistent package manager usage across environments. Enable Corepack by setting the `ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_COREPACK` environment variable to `1`.

```json
{
  "packageManager": "pnpm@7.5.1"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel CLI with pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/cms/agility-cms

Install the Vercel CLI package manager using pnpm. This tool is required to pull environment variables from Vercel to your local project during development and deployment setup.

```bash
pnpm i vercel
```

--------------------------------

### Configure pnpm engine version in package.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/errors/error-list

Specifies the required pnpm version and package manager version in package.json to resolve ERR_PNPM_UNSUPPORTED_ENGINE errors. This configuration ensures the correct pnpm version is used during deployment and local development.

```json
{
  "engines": {
    "pnpm": "^7.5.1"
  },
  "packageManager": "pnpm@7.5.1"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel SDK using pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/sdk

This command installs the `@vercel/sdk` package into your project's dependencies using pnpm. It is the first step to enable access to Vercel's REST API resources within your application.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/sdk
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize new LangChain project directory with pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/ai-gateway/framework-integrations/langchain

This command sequence creates a new directory for a LangChain project, navigates into it, and initializes a new project using `pnpm dlx init -y` for quick setup.

```bash
mkdir langchain-ai-gateway
cd langchain-ai-gateway
pnpm dlx init -y
```

--------------------------------

### Run Conformance audit with pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Executes the Conformance `audit` command using `pnpm dlx` to check code without requiring prior installation of NPM dependencies. This is suitable for reviewing code in repositories where direct modification or build processes are not feasible.

```shell
pnpm --package=@vercel-private/conformance dlx vercel-conformance audit
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenTelemetry packages with pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Install the @vercel/otel wrapper package along with OpenTelemetry API for JavaScript/TypeScript projects. This is the simplified approach for Vercel users.

```bash
pnpm i @opentelemetry/api @vercel/otel
```

--------------------------------

### Upgrade Vercel Code Owners Package with pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

This command is used to update the `@vercel-private/code-owners` package to its latest available version using the pnpm package manager. The `--latest` flag ensures the newest version is fetched, and `--recursive` updates all dependencies within the project's monorepo structure. This is typically used for maintenance and security updates.

```pnpm
pnpm update --latest --recursive @vercel-private/code-owners
```

--------------------------------

### Store Conformance audit results to file using pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Runs the Conformance `audit` command via `pnpm dlx` and redirects its `stderr` output to a specified file, `/tmp/conformance-results.txt`. This allows for capturing and reviewing audit findings persistently without modifying the original repository.

```shell
pnpm --package=@vercel-private/conformance dlx vercel-conformance audit
&> /tmp/conformance-results.txt
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenTelemetry SDK packages with pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Install comprehensive OpenTelemetry SDK packages including node SDK, resources, semantic conventions, span processors, and OTLP HTTP exporter for manual SDK configuration.

```bash
pnpm i @opentelemetry/api @opentelemetry/sdk-node @opentelemetry/resources @opentelemetry/semantic-conventions @opentelemetry/sdk-trace-base @opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-http @opentelemetry/sdk-trace-node
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel SDK with Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/sdk

Install the @vercel/sdk package using pnpm, npm, or yarn. Choose the package manager that matches your project setup.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/sdk
```

```bash
npm i @vercel/sdk
```

```bash
yarn add @vercel/sdk
```

--------------------------------

### Install @vercel/remote-nx Package

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos/nx

Install the @vercel/remote-nx plugin using your preferred package manager (pnpm, yarn, npm, or bun). This plugin enables remote caching functionality for Nx projects on Vercel.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/remote-nx
```

```bash
yarn i @vercel/remote-nx
```

```bash
npm i @vercel/remote-nx
```

```bash
bun i @vercel/remote-nx
```

--------------------------------

### Specify Package Manager with vercel.json installCommand

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/package-managers

This snippet demonstrates how to configure a specific package manager, such as pnpm, for a Vercel deployment using the `installCommand` property within the `vercel.json` file. This setting overrides the default package manager used by Vercel for that particular deployment.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "installCommand": "pnpm install"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel CLI Globally with pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/ecommerce/bigcommerce

Installs the Vercel Command Line Interface (CLI) globally using pnpm. The Vercel CLI is a crucial tool for managing Vercel projects directly from your local development environment, enabling operations like linking projects and managing environment variables.

```bash
pnpm i -g vercel
```

--------------------------------

### Install `jose` Package for JWT Verification

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Install the `jose` package using pnpm, a JavaScript package manager, to enable JSON Web Token (JWT) verification capabilities in your Node.js application.

```bash
pnpm i jose
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel Blob Package

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/storage/vercel-blob/server-upload

Installs the `@vercel/blob` package using various package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun). This is the initial step to enable Vercel Blob functionality within your project.

```Terminal
pnpm i @vercel/blob
```

```Terminal
yarn add @vercel/blob
```

```Terminal
npm i @vercel/blob
```

```Terminal
bun add @vercel/blob
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies with Multiple Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/private-registry

Installation commands for setting up dependencies using four different package managers: npm, yarn, pnpm, and bun. Each command installs the project's dependencies as specified in the package.json file.

```bash
npm i
```

```bash
yarn i
```

```bash
pnpm i
```

```bash
bun i
```

--------------------------------

### Install AI SDK Package via pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/ai-gateway/provider-options

Install the required AI SDK package as a dependency for your project. This is the first step needed to use Vercel AI Gateway provider options in your application.

```bash
pnpm install ai
```

--------------------------------

### Install LaunchDarkly Vercel Server SDK across package managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/edge-config/integrations/launchdarkly-edge-config

Commands to install LaunchDarkly's Vercel Server SDK using pnpm, yarn, npm, and bun. Required for implementing feature flag functionality in Vercel middleware.

```shell
pnpm i @launchdarkly/vercel-server-sdk
```

```shell
yarn add @launchdarkly/vercel-server-sdk
```

```shell
npm i @launchdarkly/vercel-server-sdk
```

```shell
bun add @launchdarkly/vercel-server-sdk
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Configure @nuxt/image Module

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/image-optimization/quickstart

Install the @nuxt/image package using package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, or bun) and add it to the Nuxt config modules array. This enables image optimization for Nuxt applications.

```bash
pnpm i @nuxt/image
```

```bash
yarn i @nuxt/image
```

```bash
npm i @nuxt/image
```

```bash
bun i @nuxt/image
```

--------------------------------

### Create Next.js Project with Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/edge-config/integrations/devcycle-edge-config

Create a new Next.js project using multiple package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun). Required for setting up a Vercel project with DevCycle integration.

```bash
pnpm create next-app@latest
```

```bash
yarn create next-app@latest
```

```bash
npx create-next-app@latest
```

```bash
bunx create-next-app@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Install @vercel/remix package with package managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/frameworks/full-stack/remix

Install the @vercel/remix package using pnpm, yarn, npm, or bun. This package exposes useful types and utilities for Remix apps deployed on Vercel, such as json, defer, and createCookie functions.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/remix
```

```bash
yarn i @vercel/remix
```

```bash
npm i @vercel/remix
```

```bash
bun i @vercel/remix
```

--------------------------------

### Install `@vercel/related-projects` NPM Package

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/monorepos

Install the `@vercel/related-projects` package using your preferred package manager (npm, bun, yarn, or pnpm). This package provides utility functions for easily accessing and parsing information about related projects within your Vercel deployments.

```bash
npm i @vercel/related-projects
```

```bash
bun add @vercel/related-projects
```

```bash
yarn add @vercel/related-projects
```

```bash
pnpm add @vercel/related-projects
```

--------------------------------

### Start Next.js Development Server with Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/contentful

Install dependencies and start the Next.js development server with pnpm, yarn, npm, or bun. The application will run on http://localhost:3000 and support hot module reloading for development.

```pnpm
pnpm install && pnpm run dev
```

```yarn
yarn && yarn dev
```

```npm
npm install && npm run dev
```

```bun
bun install && bun run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install @vercel/remix package

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Install the `@vercel/remix` package using pnpm to access Vercel-specific utilities for Remix applications, such as enhanced `json`, `defer`, and `createCookie` functions. This package replaces standard Remix utility imports for better Vercel feature integration.

```shell
pnpm i @vercel/remix
```

--------------------------------

### Install project dependencies (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun)

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/ai-gateway/framework-integrations/langchain

These commands install project dependencies using various package managers. Although presented under 'Running the application', these are primarily for ensuring all required packages are installed before execution.

```bash
pnpm i
```

```bash
yarn i
```

```bash
npm i
```

```bash
bun i
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Vercel Sandbox Project with npm/pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/vercel-sandbox/reference/readme

Set up a new Vercel Sandbox project by creating a directory, initializing package.json, and installing the @vercel/sandbox SDK along with required dependencies. This establishes the foundation for running isolated code environments.

```shell
mkdir sandbox-test
cd sandbox-test
pnpm init
pnpm add @vercel/sandbox ms
pnpm add -D @types/ms @types/node
```

--------------------------------

### Run Next.js development server

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/sign-in-with-vercel/getting-started

Commands to start the Next.js development server using different package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun). The application will be available at http://localhost:3000 for local testing.

```bash
pnpm run dev
```

```bash
yarn run dev
```

```bash
npm run dev
```

```bash
bun run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install BotID package with package managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/botid/get-started

Install the BotID package using different package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, or bun). This is the first step to add bot protection to your Vercel project.

```bash
pnpm i botid
```

```bash
yarn i botid
```

```bash
npm i botid
```

```bash
bun i botid
```

--------------------------------

### Install @vercel/functions package for Astro TypeScript middleware

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/frameworks/frontend/astro

Install the @vercel/functions package required for TypeScript support in Vercel edge middleware. Supports multiple package managers including pnpm, yarn, npm, and bun.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/functions
```

```bash
yarn i @vercel/functions
```

```bash
npm i @vercel/functions
```

```bash
bun i @vercel/functions
```

--------------------------------

### Install Astro Vercel Adapter

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/frameworks/frontend/astro

Install the @astrojs/vercel package using various package managers (pnpm, yarn, npm, bun). This adapter enables server-rendered Astro apps and Vercel features on static sites.

```bash
pnpm i @astrojs/vercel
```

```bash
yarn i @astrojs/vercel
```

```bash
npm i @astrojs/vercel
```

```bash
bun i @astrojs/vercel
```
